method_atom: a systematic classification technique that enumerates a complete set of inequivalent concrete representations of an abstract algebraic structure as first-order differential operators acting on a finite-dimensional space, exploiting distinguished invariant subobjects (subobjects preserved under all structure-respecting automorphisms) to organize the enumeration and factor out equivalence.
object_atom: real Lie algebras of dimension at most four, together with their realizations as vector fields on a space of an arbitrary finite number of variables, and their associated automorphisms, differentiations, ideals, and subalgebras.
brief_reason: the invariant-subobject-driven classification and realization-enumeration machinery is the transferable method, while the specific low-dimensional real Lie algebras realized as vector fields are the domain-specific object.
